21.correct: B.N. Rau was the Constitutional Adviser to the Constituent Assembly; he prepared the initial draft of the Constitution on which the Drafting Committee worked.

wrong.B: Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar was a distinguished member of the Drafting Committee, not the Constitutional Adviser.
wrong.C: T.T. Krishnamachari was a member of the Drafting Committee.
wrong.D: K.M. Munshi was also a member of the Drafting Committee, not the Adviser.

21.fact: The Drafting Committee was chaired by Dr B.R. Ambedkar; B.N. Rau later became a judge of the International Court of Justice.

22.correct: Union Territories are administered by the President of India acting through an Administrator (such as a Lieutenant Governor) appointed by him, under Article 239.

wrong.A: Parliament can legislate for UTs but does not administer them day to day.
wrong.B: Only some UTs with legislatures have a Chief Minister; administration is ultimately through the President's appointee.
wrong.C: A Governor heads a State, not a Union Territory.

22.fact: Delhi and Puducherry are UTs with their own legislatures and Chief Ministers, but still have a Lieutenant Governor.

23.correct: The Santhal dance is performed by the Santhal tribe (eastern India) mainly during harvest festivals and religious/community ceremonies, celebrating nature and community life.

wrong.B: It is a tribal cultural dance, not performed for political rallies.
wrong.C: It is not restricted only to weddings.
wrong.D: It is a celebratory dance, not a funeral performance.

23.fact: The Santhals are one of India's largest tribal communities, spread across Jharkhand, West Bengal and Odisha.

24.correct: The Tippani dance of Gujarat is traditionally performed by women on festive and celebratory occasions such as weddings and festivals.

wrong.B: It is not limited to religious rituals only.
wrong.C: It is not performed only during the harvest season.
wrong.D: It is a folk celebration, not a political-rally performance.

24.fact: The dance originated from women labourers who beat the floor with long sticks (tippani) while working.

25.correct: The Satpura Range runs east-west through central India (Madhya Pradesh and adjoining areas), lying between the Narmada and Tapti rivers.

wrong.A: Northern India is dominated by the Himalayas and the Gangetic plain, not the Satpura.
wrong.B: Southern India has the Deccan Plateau and the Western/Eastern Ghats.
wrong.D: Eastern India has the Chotanagpur Plateau and eastern hills, not the Satpura.

25.fact: The Satpura Range separates the Narmada valley to its north from the Tapti valley to its south.

26.correct: The Ganga-Brahmaputra delta lies mainly in West Bengal (and Bangladesh); within India it is located in West Bengal and forms the Sundarbans.

wrong.B: Uttar Pradesh lies in the upper Ganga plain, not the delta.
wrong.C: Bihar is in the middle Ganga plain, upstream of the delta.
wrong.D: Assam lies along the Brahmaputra valley, not the combined delta.

26.fact: The Ganga-Brahmaputra delta is the world's largest delta and hosts the Sundarbans mangrove forest.

27.correct: Dr Shankar Dayal Sharma served as President of India from 1992 to 1997 and had a distinguished academic background in constitutional law.

wrong.A: R. Venkataraman was President from 1987 to 1992, just before Sharma.
wrong.C: K.R. Narayanan was President from 1997 to 2002, after Sharma.
wrong.D: Giani Zail Singh was President from 1982 to 1987.

27.fact: Shankar Dayal Sharma had been a law teacher and earlier served as Vice-President of India (1987-92).

28.correct: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a composite index based on three dimensions: education (knowledge), income (standard of living) and life expectancy (a long, healthy life).

wrong.B: HDI does not combine GDP, GNI and literacy in that specific set; income is captured by GNI per capita while knowledge uses schooling, not just literacy.
wrong.C: Employment, social security and gender equality are not the three HDI components.
wrong.D: Environment is not a component of the standard HDI.

28.fact: The HDI was introduced by the UNDP in 1990, conceptualised by economists Mahbub ul Haq and Amartya Sen.

29.correct: Over the roughly 41,000-year Milankovitch cycle, Earth's axial tilt (obliquity) varies between about 22.1 and 24.5 degrees; a greater tilt increases the contrast between seasons (hotter summers, colder winters).

wrong.B: A change in axial tilt does not change the Earth's orbital speed.
wrong.C: Obliquity affects climate/seasons, not the planet's magnetic field.
wrong.D: There is a clear climatic effect, so 'no effect' is wrong.

29.fact: Milankovitch cycles - changes in eccentricity, obliquity and precession - drive long-term ice-age cycles.

30.correct: The French Quarter (White Town) of Puducherry is built primarily in the Indo-French colonial style, with grid streets, pastel villas, verandahs and tree-lined boulevards.

wrong.B: Gothic Revival is a European church-architecture style, seen in some churches but not the quarter's overall character.
wrong.C: Art Deco is a 20th-century style, not the defining style of the French Quarter.
wrong.D: Neoclassical elements appear in some buildings, but the quarter is characterised as Indo-French colonial.

30.fact: Puducherry (Pondicherry) was a French colony until 1954, which shaped its distinctive urban layout.

31.correct: Orion is a constellation - a recognisable pattern of stars in the night sky, prominent in winter and known for the three stars of 'Orion's Belt'.

wrong.B: Orion is not a single star; it contains many stars such as Betelgeuse and Rigel.
wrong.C: Orion is not a galaxy; it is a group of stars within our own Milky Way galaxy.

31.fact: The Orion Nebula, a stellar nursery, lies in this constellation and is visible to the naked eye.

32.correct: India first hosted (co-hosted) the Cricket World Cup in 1987 (the Reliance Cup), shared with Pakistan - the first World Cup held outside England.

wrong.A: 1975 was the first-ever World Cup, held in England.
wrong.B: 1983 was the year India won the World Cup, but it was played in England.
wrong.D: 1996 was the next subcontinent World Cup (India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka), not the first time India hosted.

32.fact: India later co-hosted the 2011 World Cup and won it, and hosted the 2023 World Cup solo.

33.correct: Wilhelm Steinitz is regarded as the first official World Chess Champion, holding the title from 1886 to 1894.

wrong.A: Bobby Fischer became World Champion in 1972, nearly a century later.
wrong.C: Garry Kasparov was World Champion from 1985, in the modern era.
wrong.D: Paul Morphy was a dominant unofficial player of the 1850s but was never the official world champion.

33.fact: Steinitz is also credited with developing the principles of positional (strategic) chess.

34.correct: The heartbeat is an involuntary action - it occurs automatically and continuously without conscious control, driven by the heart's own pacemaker.

wrong.A: A reflex action is a sudden, automatic response to a stimulus (e.g. a knee-jerk), not the continuous heartbeat.
wrong.C: A voluntary action is one we consciously control, such as walking.
wrong.D: 'Coordinated action' is not the standard classification for the heartbeat.

34.fact: The heartbeat is initiated by the sino-atrial (SA) node, the heart's natural pacemaker, and regulated by the autonomic nervous system.

35.correct: The primary function of the lysosome is intracellular waste disposal - it contains digestive enzymes that break down worn-out cell parts, food particles and foreign material.

wrong.A: Energy production is the function of the mitochondria.
wrong.C: Protein synthesis is carried out by ribosomes.
wrong.D: Lipid storage is not the lysosome's primary role.

35.fact: Because they digest cellular debris and can break down the cell itself, lysosomes are nicknamed the cell's 'suicide bags'.

36.correct: Chilika Lake in Odisha is India's largest brackish (saltwater) lake and the largest coastal lagoon in the country.

wrong.A: Vembanad Lake (Kerala) is the longest lake in India but Chilika is the largest brackish-water lake.
wrong.B: Pulicat Lake is the second-largest brackish lagoon, smaller than Chilika.
wrong.D: Sambhar Lake (Rajasthan) is the largest inland salt (saline) lake, but Chilika is the largest brackish coastal lake.

36.fact: Chilika is a Ramsar wetland famous for migratory birds and the Irrawaddy dolphin.

37.correct: In November 2024, Australia became the first country to pass a law banning social-media use for children under 16.

wrong.A: India has discussed age regulation but did not pass such a first-of-its-kind law.
wrong.B: The USA has state-level debates but no such national ban.
wrong.C: Japan did not pass this pioneering law.

37.fact: Australia's law puts the responsibility on social-media platforms to keep under-16 users off their services.

38.correct: The First Anglo-Mysore War was fought in 1767-1769 between the British East India Company and Haidar Ali of Mysore, ending with the Treaty of Madras.

wrong.D: 1799 was the Fourth (final) Anglo-Mysore War, in which Tipu Sultan died.

38.fact: Haidar Ali nearly captured Madras in the First War, forcing the British into the humiliating Treaty of Madras (1769).

39.correct: In the 1946 provincial elections, the Indian National Congress performed very well in the 'general' (non-Muslim) constituencies, winning a large majority of those seats.

wrong.B: The Justice Party had declined by 1946 and was not a major winner.
wrong.C: The Communist Party of India won only a handful of seats.
wrong.D: The Swaraj Party was a 1920s formation and no longer a separate force in 1946.

39.fact: The Muslim League won most of the Muslim-reserved seats in 1946, strengthening its demand for Pakistan.

40.correct: Indira Gandhi was the first woman to receive the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1971.

wrong.A: Aruna Asaf Ali received the Bharat Ratna in 1997, much later.
wrong.B: Lata Mangeshkar received the Bharat Ratna in 2001.
wrong.D: Mother Teresa received the Nobel Peace Prize (1979) but not the Bharat Ratna.

40.fact: The Bharat Ratna was instituted in 1954; Indira Gandhi received it while serving as Prime Minister.
